SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.vxml
Interface VxmlDialog

All Superinterfaces:
Joinable, JoinableDialog, JoinEventNotifier, MediaEventNotifier<VxmlDialogEvent>, MediaObject
public interface VxmlDialog
extends JoinableDialog, MediaObject, MediaEventNotifier<VxmlDialogEvent>

The interface to delegate the execution of a given vxml script..
A VxmlDialog can be joined to any other Joinable, like a NetworkConnection or MediaMixer
Note: A VxmlDialog is not a ResourceContainer (you cannot set RTCs, for example).

Nested Class Summary

static Parameter AUTO_START
          If set to Boolean.TRUE, the dialog will start immediately and automatically when it is prepared.
static Parameter CONNECTION_DATA
          This optional parameter provides the script with the signalling message, or any other signalling data pertaining to the remote connection.
static Parameter DOCUMENT_MAX_AGE
          Indicates that the file is willing to use content whose age is no greater than the specified time in seconds (cf. max-age in HTTP 1.1 [RFC2616]).
static Parameter DOCUMENT_MAX_STALE
          Indicates that the file is willing to use content that has exceeded its expiration time (cf. max-stale in HTTP 1.1 [RFC2616]).

          The interval in ms to wait for the content to be returned before throwing an MediaErr.TIMEOUT event.

Field Detail

FETCH_TIMEOUT

static final Parameter FETCH_TIMEOUT
The interval in ms to wait for the content to be returned before throwing an MediaErr.TIMEOUT event.

DOCUMENT_MAX_STALE

static final Parameter DOCUMENT_MAX_STALE
Indicates that the file is willing to use content that has exceeded its expiration time (cf. max-stale in HTTP 1.1 [RFC2616]). If maxstale is assigned a value, then the file is willing to accept content that has exceeded its expiration time by no more than the specified number of seconds.

DOCUMENT_MAX_AGE

static final Parameter DOCUMENT_MAX_AGE
Indicates that the file is willing to use content whose age is no greater than the specified time in seconds (cf. max-age in HTTP 1.1 [RFC2616]). The file is not willing to use stale content, unless maxstale is also provided.

AUTO_START

static final Parameter AUTO_START
If set to Boolean.TRUE, the dialog will start immediately and automatically when it is prepared. The application does not need to call the start(java.util.Map) method.
In any case, both VxmlDialogEvent.PREPARED and VxmlDialogEvent.STARTED will be received.

CONNECTION_DATA

static final Parameter CONNECTION_DATA
This optional parameter provides the script with the signalling message, or any other signalling data pertaining to the remote connection.
In a SIP context, this would be the (raw) SIP message.
Value is a String

LOCAL_URI

static final java.lang.String LOCAL_URI
The session variable that defines the local uri (as per https://datatracker.ietf.org/drafts/draft-burke-vxml/ ).
These can be used as keys in the Map argument of start(java.util.Map) or prepare(java.net.URL, javax.media.mscontrol.Parameters, java.util.Map).

Constant Field Values
Method Detail

prepare

void prepare(java.net.URL vxmlScript,
             Parameters options,
             java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> sessionVariables)
Request the preparation of the execution of the given vxml script.
This usually involves fetching and parsing the script.
The event VxmlDialogEvent.PREPARED is returned when the preparation is complete.

Parameters:
vxmlScript - URL to the vxml script
options - See the various features in this interface
sessionVariables - see start(Map)
Throws:
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the dialog has been released

prepare

void prepare(java.lang.String inlinedScript,
             Parameters options,
             java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> sessionVariables)
Same as prepare(URL, Parameters, Map), but the vxml script is provided in line, as the first argument.
The rest of the processing (events, etc) is unchanged.

start

void start(java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> sessionVariables)
Start a prepared vxml dialog.
The event VxmlDialogEvent.STARTED is returned when the dialog has started.

Parameters:
sessionVariables - A namelist of dialog session variables.
Session variables allow to use a single vxml script, and yet customize its execution.
Some session variables have names defined in this interface.
Example usage: sessionVariables.put(VxmlDialog.REMOTE_URI, "sip:mark@172.17.8.131");
Other session variables can be added freely by the application.
Note: if prepare was called with AUTO_START set to true, then this method is not needed and should not be called.
Throws:
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the dialog has been released

terminate

void terminate(boolean immediate)
Request dialog termination.

Parameters:
immediate - if true, the dialog terminates immediately and does not send any further event.
Throws:
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the dialog has been released

acceptEvent

void acceptEvent(java.lang.String eventName,
                 java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> values)
Request the VxmlDialog to take a new event (from the JSR 309 application).

Parameters:
eventName -
values -
Throws:
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the dialog has been released
